characters.name: 46th Earth King
characters.description: 46th Earth KingBiographical informationNationalityBa Sing Se,Earth KingdomEthnicityEarth KingdomResidenceEarth Kingdom Royal Palace,Upper Ringof Ba Sing SePhysical descriptionHair colorWhitePersonal informationPronounsHe/him[1]AlliesRoyal Earthbender Guards, Earth KingdomEnemiesPeasantsof Ba Sing SePolitical informationPositionEarth KingPredecessor45th Earth Monarch, eventuallyYi MingSuccessor47th Earth Monarch, eventuallyJialunAffiliationEarth KingdomHou-Ting dynastyChronological informationFirst appearanceEscape from the Spirit World(only appearance)Image gallery (2)The46th Earth Kingwas therulerof theEarth Kingdomsometime during the era of Kyoshi. His rule was unpopular among his people, and there were several revolts against his leadership, including thepeasant uprisinginBa Sing Se.[1][2]After yielding to pressure byAvatarKyoshito reform his style of governance, the Earth King ultimately helped to draft a new constitution for the Earth Kingdom, which transformed the country into a constitutional monarchy and brought considerable progress and prosperity to its people.[3][4] Strong-willed and imperious, the Earth King had little support from his people, and his reign was marked by many challenges to his rule which severely tested theEarth Kingdom's government.[1] The Earth Kingdom faced a rebellion led byChinthe Conqueror, a warlord from the country's far northwest. Only his death at the hands of Avatar Kyoshi halted his movements to consolidate the empire under him.[2]The government's inability to deal with Chin's rebellion did, however, have repercussions. The massive losses the Earth Kingdom sustained led the citizens to realize many of the government's failures, and popular sentiment grew into the belief that the monarchy was outdated and did not support the people's interests. In Ba Sing Se, this belief led to a peasant uprising, with thousands storming theUpper Ringand destroying anything that represented the "old government". They eventually surrounded the Earth King'spalaceand demanded he be removed from his position.[1] The 46th Earth King was a domineering man who refused to change the way his monarchy operated despite his subjects' objections.[1]He was indifferent to his people's revolts, choosing to have Kyoshi put an end to them instead of helping them, and tried to get her arrested when she asked him to listen to the population's demands. It was only after Kyoshi took down his guards and threatened him that he finally decided to take action and improve the state of his country.[3][4]

Sweeney(inThe Legend of Korra)Davis Pak(inQuest for Balance)Eric Nam(inAvatar Aang: The Last Airbender)[8][9]Image gallery (761)Aangwas anAir Nomadborn in 12BGand theAvatarduring theHundred Year War, succeeding AvatarRoku[10]and preceding AvatarKorra. As the Avatar of his time, he was the only person capable of using all fourbending arts:airbending,waterbending,earthbending, andfirebending. He was also one of a select few Avatars and one of the first in manycycles[11]to learn the ancient art ofenergybendingas well as the first Avatar known to have actively used the technique. Shortly before the beginning of the Hundred Year War, Aang was frozen in an iceberg for a hundred years; he later emerged, still biologically twelve years old, into aworldengulfed by war.[3]During his absence, theFire Nationhad waged war upon the othernationsand managed tocompletely wipe outthe pacifistic Air Nomads. It fell to Aang, the Avatar and the last airbender, to end the War by mastering the other three elements and defeatingFire LordOzai. He remained a kind and goofy kid at heart throughout his year-long struggle, despite the overwhelming loss of his people and the heavy burdens he was forced to bear. After hisvictoryover thePhoenix King, Aang began a romantic relationship with his close friend,Katara.[12]The couple eventually married and raised a family of three children:Bumi, the oldest, a nonbender who became an airbender later in life;Kya, a waterbender; and the youngest,Tenzin, an airbender. characters.personality_section: Aang was fun-loving, somewhat naive, and adventurous with a sense of humor. He possessed deep respect for life and freedom, refused to eat meat, and was often reluctant to fight in his younger years.[11]He craved the stimulation of new people and places. His frequent off-course detours frustrated both allies and pursuers alike. He typically wished to forget his duties as the Avatar and would long for the normality that others possessed.[96]Yet Aang had always prided himself on a complex social network of friends extending over all four nations, and war did not stand in his way. In addition, he looked forward to playing with all the exoticfaunain each place he visited.[25]Whether it wasotter penguins,[3]hopping llamas,hog monkeys, orgiant koi fish,[96]no fit animal Aang saw went un-ridden. Within this carefree exterior, however, Aang hid a great deal of guilt and a heavy mental burden in his duties as Avatar. As the reluctant hero, he wished he had been there to help his people a century earlier.[1]This initially caused him to conceal his true identity from friends,[25]and he still had a tendency to slack off in his studies of the bending arts, even though he excelled at them.[1][97]He also tended to become distressed when he saw the destruction caused by the Hundred Year War, believing that as the Avatar, it was his job to prevent such events from happening.[27]Eventually, Aang decided to stop dwelling on the past;[14]however, on several occasions, his desire to end the War c

characters.name: Aang
characters.description: AangBiographical informationNationalitySouthern Air Temple,Air NomadsEthnicityAir NomadAge112(biologically 12 inAvatar: The Last Airbender)Born12BGPhysical descriptionHair colorBlack(typically shaven)Personal informationPronounsHe/him[1]AlliesKya,SokkaEnemiesZuko,Fire NationWeapon of choiceAir,glider staffFightingstylesAirbending,Avatar StatePolitical informationProfessionAvatarMonkPositionAvatar-in-trainingAffiliationAir NomadsTeam AvatarChronological informationFirst appearanceUnaired pilot(only appearance)Voiced byMitchel Musso Aangwas a maleAir Nomad, the immediate successor ofAvatarRoku, and the predecessor of AvatarKorra. He was fun-loving, somewhat naive and adventurous. He already seemed to have a conscience against killing people, as he savedPrinceZukofrom falling from a giant statue, saving him from a possible death. He already had somewaterbendingexperience, as he bent the water and both correctedKya's attempts and gave her advice. Aang and his friends, Kya andSokka, were fleeing from Zuko, who was hunting them down fromhis ship. They ran into asea serpent, but it took interest in the ship after Zuko attacked it, allowing the trio to escape to an island. While there, Kya practiced her waterbending, but could not control it well; Aang offered her some advice and demonstrated the correct leg stance. When Sokka demanded they help search forfood, Kya was reluctant to leave Aang, as he was "the world's last hope"; Sokka went off alone in a huff. Kya and Aang remained at the camp and engaged in a slightly awkward conversation beforeMomoalerted them of an upcoming danger.

characters.description: AdakiBiographical informationNationalityKarr Lok Island,Fire Nation[2]EthnicityFire NationAge11 inLight It Up![3]Physical descriptionHair colorBlackSkin colorBrownPersonal informationPronounsHe/himAlliesJiyung,Aera,Sujun,ChuanEnemiesTeam UndefeatedWeapon of choiceFireFighting styleFirebendingPolitical informationProfessionStudentPositionRegionalbendingtournament championAffiliationSunray AcademyChronological informationFirst appearanceChapter One (Light It Up!)Last appearanceChapter Fifteen (Light It Up!)Image gallery (7)Adakiwas afirebenderduring theera of Szeto. Born to a family offishersonKarr Lok Island, he dreamed of becoming abendingchampion and master. To achieve this dream, he founded Team Matchsticks atSunray Academy, eventually leading the group to win a regional firebending tournament. Adaki was born to Adaro onKarr Lok Islandin theFire Nation[2]during the era of Szeto.[4]His family traditionally worked as fire-fishers, using their firebending to attractfishto their boats at night. Adaki's father Adaro was quite proficient at this work, regularly impressing him.[2]Though the young boy liked fishing, he also considered one of his grandfather as "pretty cool"; the latter had gone against tradition to become a farmer and thus become the "wild rebel within his family".[5] As Adaki grew up, he went to Karr Lok Island'sschool,Sunray Academy, where he made three friends:Jiyung,Aera, andSujun.[4]He came to adore the "Flame Throwers", a youth team of local firebenders who had won the nationwide bending competition. Adaki sought to emulate his father and the "Flame Throwers", hoping to become a more powerful firebender.[2]

characters.name: Aera
characters.description: AeraBiographical informationNationalityKarr Lok Island,Fire NationEthnicityFire NationAge11 inLight It Up!Physical descriptionEye colorGrayHair colorBlackSkin colorBrownPersonal informationPronounsShe/her[1]AlliesBuddy,Adaki,Sujun,Jiyung,ChuanEnemiesTeam UndefeatedWeapon of choiceFireFighting styleFirebendingPolitical informationProfessionStudentPositionRegionalbendingtournament championAffiliationSunray AcademyTeam MatchsticksChronological informationFirst appearanceChapter Two (Light It Up!)Image gallery (6)Aerawas afirebenderduring the lateera of Szeto. As a student ofSunray AcademyonKarr Lok Island, she became a member of Team Matchsticks and won a regionalbendingtournament. Aera was known for her unique firebending style which was based on herdisability. Aera was born on Karr Lok Island of theFire Nation. She was raised by her grandmother and lacked her left arm below the elbow,[1][2]hindering her as she began to learn how to use her bending abilities. At first, Aera was trained by her grandmother on the beach near their house, focusing on leg-based firebending techniques. She struggled with traditional forms which usually required two arms.[3] Aera initially had difficulties in making friends, and often felt isolated despite her strong bond to her grandmother. As a result of her disability, she was occasionally bullied by a group of older children consisting of Tearan, Moriko, Akito, and Botan.[3][4]The bullies observed her firebending training and mocked her whenever she made mistakes. Hoping to get better, Aera began to train with her shortened left arm at night, producing a delicate fire ribbon but nothing else.[3]

characters.name: Afiko
characters.description: AfikoBiographical informationNationalitySouthern Air TempleEthnicityAir NomadDied5AGPhysical descriptionHair colorBrownPersonal informationEnemiesAang, Air Nomads,Sozin,Fire NationWeapon of choiceAirFighting styleAirbendingPolitical informationProfessionMonkPositionAirbending masterTraitorAffiliationFire NationChronological informationFirst appearanceAvatar: The Last AirbenderTrading Card Game(only appearance)Image gallery (9)Afiko, known asthe Betrayer, was anairbenderfrom theSouthern Air Templewho revealed the location of his home to theFire Nation, after which the country'sarmyraided it during theAir Nomad Genocide. Made notorious by this betrayal, Afiko was charged with treason in 5AGand later executed by decree ofFire LordSozin. Born in the Southern Air Temple, Afiko was seemingly normal growing up, but he grew jealous whenAang's identity as theAvatarwas announced. Envious, Afiko betrayed his people by revealing the temple's location to the Fire Nation.[1] Fire Nation soldiers stormed the temple and slaughtered the other monks, but they were too late to catch Aang, who had run away from home shortly before the assault. Afiko was instrumental in engineering the genocide of his fellowAir Nomads, earning a place as Fire Lord Sozin's close adviser. He also seemed to have aided Sozin in attacking theEarth Kingdom, in that he is often shown blocking or dispersingearthbendingattacks and attacking earthbenders. The traitorous monk died long before Aang's return and the subsequent undermining of all Afiko's work. In the end, despite his loyalty to the Fire Nation and his achievements, historical records indicate that Afiko met his demise in the war's fifth year, as Fire Lord Sozin had him killed for being a traitor.[1]

characters.description: AikaBiographical informationNationalitySuki's village,Earth KingdomEthnicityKyoshi IslanderPhysical descriptionEye colorBlue[1]Hair colorBlack[2]Skin colorLightPersonal informationAlliesKyoshi Warriors,Team Avatar,Wen,Fire NationEnemiesNew Ozai Society,AzulaWeapon of choiceWar fans,katanaFightingstylesTessenjutsu,chi-blockingPolitical informationProfessionKyoshi WarriorAffiliationKyoshi WarriorsKyoshi IslandChronological informationFirst appearance"Appa's Lost Days"Last appearanceThe Kyoshi WarriorstrilogyImage gallery (7)Aika[3]was aKyoshi Warriorwho lived onKyoshi Islandby the lateHundred Year War. She was one ofSuki's followers, accompanying her during a series of aid missions in theEarth Kingdominspired byAvatarAang. After the Great War's conclusion, Aika helpedFire LordZukoas part ofcounter-insurgency operationsin theFire Nation. Aika grew up inSuki's villageon Kyoshi Island during the late Hundred Year War. She experiencedTeam Avatar's visit andZuko's raid on Kyoshi Islandin 99AG, helping to rebuild her home village afterward. When the leader of her Kyoshi Warrior team, Suki, asked her comrades whether they were willing to venture to the Earth Kingdom mainland to help others just as Avatar Aang did, Aika volunteered alongsideRie,Fume, andMira.[4] After leaving their home per ship, one of the team's first destinations wasRazor Reefin the southernMo Ce Sea. There, the group sought a localmartial artsmaster named Tidao, only to discover that the latter had long left the location. Yet they used the opportunity for a friendly sparring session with some local adventurers who were guarding an archaeologist,Wen, at Razor Reef. They then continued their journey to the mainland.[5]

characters.name: Aiko
characters.description: AikoBiographical informationNationalityRepublic City,United RepublicPersonal informationWeapon of choiceAirFighting styleAirbendingPolitical informationProfessionStunt professionalPositionAssistant toBreezeAffiliationVarrimovers InternationalChronological informationFirst appearanceMovers & Shakers(only appearance)Aikowas a stunt professional employed byVarrimovers International. She developedairbendingabilities following theHarmonic Convergenceof 171AG, which caused her to use her bending to act as an assistant to "stuntmaster"Breeze— who claimed to be the actual airbender.[1] Aiko worked as a member of the stunt crew at Varrimovers International. She loved working on the one or twomoversshe had experience with, until she caused an accident upon discovering her new airbending abilities. She was incredibly grateful that her coworker, Rai Feng, took credit for the accident. However, he turned it into an entire new brand, with Aiko being a part of it. He rebranded himself as "Breeze", a legendary "stuntmaster" with a brand new airbending technique, while Aiko was actually performing the stunts.[2] Aiko never wanted to be an airbender, as she loved the mover industry too much. She was scared of being discovered by anyone, as she believed that she would be forced to join theAir Nation— which in turn would mean that she would have new duties and obligations that would carry her away from the industry she loved.[1][2]However, Aiko had only managed to obtain a tenuous grasp on her bending, and she still caused accidents periodically when she got spooked or startled. The situation threatened to spiral out of control with the stunt team working on the production ofSengo: Lady of the Winds, which had an airbender protagonist, meaning that filming relied on Aiko's bending.[2]During the production of the mover, Aiko stayed at theSleeping Sloth-Mouse, an hotel close to the studio.[3] Aiko was someone who can easily get scared or worried, especially when it came to her airbending being discovered. She was desperate not to leave the mover industry, as she loved her job in the stunt crew.[2][1]
characters.personality_section: Aiko was someone who can easily get scared or worried, especially when it came to her airbending being discovered. She was desperate not to leave the mover industry, as she loved her job in the stunt crew.[2][1]

characters.name: Aiko
characters.description: AikoBiographical informationNationalityJasmine IslandEthnicityJasmine IslanderPersonal informationPronounsShe/her[1]AlliesAkataa,Basalt,Siku,Fu Meili,Lu TenEnemiesYueya,ZhaoWeapon of choiceFireFighting styleFirebendingPolitical informationProfessionSoldierPositionJasmine Island protectorAffiliationJasmine Island protectorsChronological informationFirst appearanceUncle Iroh's Adventure Guide-Vengeance & Virtue(only appearance)Aikowas a member ofJasmine Island'sprotectorsduring the lateHundred Year War. She helped to defend the island's people from theFire Nationmilitaryduring "The Devastation". Aiko was a born into a family with a proud martial tradition, as her ancestors had traditionally served as protectors for many generations. When the Hundred Year War erupted, however, the family's values began to shift; the clan increasingly regarded it as their duty to not just shield Jasmine Island at home, but to venture across the world to fight the Fire Nation's tyranny to prevent its aggression from ever reaching their home. As Aiko grew up, she saw her aunts and uncles, her parents, and finally her older siblings leave the island to fight in the Great War.[2]The family's actions were not uncommon. Over the course of the Hundred Year War, it had become normal for Jasmine Island protectors to ultimately leave their home to fight as volunteers against the Fire Nation.[3] Eventually, Aiko was left as the oldest member of her family who still lived on Jasmine Island. Her relatives had charged her with protecting her little brother Chiyo. Though she resented being left behind, Aiko saw it as her duty to honor her oaths.[2]She faced true conflict when a strong Fire Nation force led byAdmiralYueyaattacked Jasmine Island, launching a murderous campaign later dubbed "The Devastation". After the initial surprise attacks led byCaptainZhao, Aiko rallied around a veteran protector namedAkataa, and tried to fight off the invaders.[1]Together with Akataa,Basalt,Siku, andFu Meili,[1][3]Aiko later encountered Fire Nation soldiers led by CaptainLu Ten, who actually opposed Yueya's rampage. The protectors allied with Lu Ten,[4][5]and together worked out a plan to stop Yueya.[4][6]This culminated in a battle where Aiko and her friends defeated the admiral, helping to save their home.[4][7] Aiko was a dutiful person who strove to protect her home and family at all costs, a duty which had been honored in her clan for generations.[1][2]
characters.personality_section: Aiko was a dutiful person who strove to protect her home and family at all costs, a duty which had been honored in her clan for generations.[1][2]

characters.name: Ailani
characters.description: AilaniBiographical informationNationalityRepublic City,United RepublicEthnicityWater TribeResidenceCentral District, Republic CityPersonal informationPronounsShe/her[1]Weapon of choiceWaterFighting styleWaterbendingPolitical informationProfessionPoliticianPositionMayor of Republic CityChronological informationFirst appearanceAvatar Legends: The Roleplaying Game-Republic City(only appearance)Ailaniwas a mayor ofRepublic Cityduring the presidency ofZhu Li Moon. She was well-known for her populist rhetoric and her desire to see real change in the city. Ailani was the daughter ofFoggy Swamp Tribeimmigrants. She grew up adapting traditionalwaterbendingto grow crops in urban environments. She saw firsthand how these community gardens sustainedpublic healthwhen rapid development and industrialization complicated access to fresh produce ormedicinalherbs. Ailani quickly earned the fondness of her fellow citizens throughout the city, and they encouraged her to vote for public office. Ailani chose to run for mayor of Republic City, although the position historically was little more than ceremonial, while theUnited Republic Council, and later thepresident, held the real power over the capital. Ailani was determined to change the state of affairs throughout her campaign. She was the clear victor in the mayoral election, having successfully garnered support across the city by establishing herself as just another citizen, without wealth or long-standing political connections. In particular, she represented real changes, which were in opposition to the interests that PresidentRaikorepresented. Despite extensive popular support, she came to office without allies in the political establishment. As a result, she knew she had to maintain her popular support to survive, and sometimes adjusted her beliefs to maintain that support.[2]

characters.name: Air Acolyte woman
characters.description: Air Acolyte womanBiographical informationNationalityRepublic City,United RepublicResidenceAir Temple IslandPhysical descriptionHair colorWhitePersonal informationAlliesTenzinand his family,Air Acolytes,Order of the White Lotus,Team AvatarEnemiesEqualists,Earth EmpirePolitical informationProfessionMidwifePositionCivilianAffiliationAir AcolytesAir Temple IslandChronological informationFirst appearance"Turning the Tides"Last appearance"Day of the Colossus"Voiced byMaria BamfordImage gallery (2)TheAir Acolyte womanwas one of the nonbendingAir Acolytesliving onAir Temple Island. She aidedPemaduringRohan's birth and subsequently left the island to escape during theEqualists'battle for Republic City.[1] When Pema went into labor during the Equalist attack on Air Temple Island, the Air Acolyte woman quickly brought her inside to give birth. As Pema began to worry for her children, the woman reassured her thatJinora,Ikki, andMeelowere safe in the room next to them and that there was nothing about which she should worry. Thanks to the Air Acolyte woman, Rohan was born with no complications. She stood by quietly asTenzinand his family gathered to see the baby. As moreEqualist airshipsapproached, she fled the island along with Tenzin's family and another female Air Acolyte, guiding her ownflying bison.[1]

characters.name: Air Nomad boy
characters.description: Air Nomad boyBiographical informationNationalitySouthern Air TempleEthnicityAir NomadDied0AGPhysical descriptionHair colorBrown(shaven)Personal informationAlliesAang, Air NomadsEnemiesFire NationWeapon of choiceAirFighting styleAirbendingPolitical informationAffiliationAir NomadsChronological informationFirst appearance"The Storm"(flashback only)Last appearance"Appa's Lost Days"(flashback only)Voiced byDaniel HansenImage gallery (2)TheAir Nomad boywas one ofAang's oldAir Nomadfriends from before theHundred Year War. Thisairbendingnomad lived in theSouthern Air Temple. He was a tall and slender boy around Aang's age. This boy, along with Aang, was among the children who went to theEastern Air Templeto choose aflying bison.[2] He was also present when Aang taught his friends an airbending technique called theair scooterand was the first to try. He successfully created the scooter, but fell on his back when he attempted to ride it. After practicing for some time, he finally mastered the trick and even created a game with his friends; however, when Aang came to play with them, the boy told him he could not, saying that because Aang was theAvatar, his team would carry an unfair advantage. This exclusion from his friends prompted Aang to sadly walk away, while this boy asked the others who wantedJinjuon their team.[1]

characters.description: Air Nomad pilgrimBiographical informationEthnicityAir NomadPhysical descriptionEye colorDark grayHair colorBlackSkin colorTanPersonal informationAlliesLaghimaWeapon of choiceAirFighting styleAirbendingPolitical informationPositionPilgrimAuthorAffiliationAir NomadsChronological informationFirst appearance"Wisdom"(only appearance)Image gallery (3)ThisAir Nomad pilgrimwas an author who sought out GuruLaghimaaround 3,829BG. The pilgrim's account of the meeting with the philosopher was later included in afamous booktitledPoems of Laghima. In their youth, the pilgrim ventured to theNorthern Air Templewith the intention of meeting Laghima, who had already become famous as a philosopher and poet. From theair temple, an Air Nomad used theirgliderto fly to anearby mountain, where the guru had opted to live alone. As the pilgrim touched down near the mountain's peak, their papers and writing utensils were scattered by the wind. The traveller panicked for a moment, but after collecting everything, they walked up the remaining way. There, an airbender met Laghima, who agreed to entertain their questions regarding wisdom as the pilgrim sat down nearby, writing down his words.

characters.name: Aisha and Layla
characters.description: Aisha and LaylaBiographical informationNationalityBa Sing Se,Earth KingdomEthnicityEarth KingdomResidenceMiddle Ring, Ba Sing SeAgeOver 16 inCity of Echoes[1]BornBefore 84AGPersonal informationPronounsShe/her (both)[1]Political informationProfessionApothecary-in-training (Layla)[2]AffiliationHu Yuan ApothecarySilver FangsChronological informationFirst appearanceCity of Echoes: Chapter ThirtyLast appearanceCity of Echoes: Chapter Thirty-OneAishaandLaylawere Dr. Jan's daughters and sisters ofXuan. They acted as delivery girls for theSilver Fangs. Layla was the future heiress toHu Yuan Apothecary. Aisha and Layla were born into a family of medical professionals inBa Sing Se.[2][3]Their mother Jan was a doctor who ran the Hu Yuan Apothecary, with the building also acting as the family home. They had a younger brother named Xuan.[4][1]In their early youth, Aisha and Layla would force Xuan to play dress-up with them. As a result, he became able to easily braid their hair.[5]When their father left Ba Sing Se to journey toTakuin an effort to find a cure to a fever affectingrefugees, he asked the three siblings to look out for each other. Tragically, their father never returned, as he was killed when his caravan was attacked by theFire Nation militaryon the way to Taku.[3] At some point, Dr. Jan decided that Layla would take over Hu Yuan Apothecary in the future.[2]Layla or Aisha also sometimes acted as delivery girls for the Silver Fangs. When one of them was sick, their route was taken up byJin.[6] Layla seemed to enjoy teasing her younger brother. She worried about his safety when she learned he would be venturing out into Fire Nation-dominated territory, but still let him go, simply asking Jin to take care of him.[1]
characters.personality_section: Layla seemed to enjoy teasing her younger brother. She worried about his safety when she learned he would be venturing out into Fire Nation-dominated territory, but still let him go, simply asking Jin to take care of him.[1]

characters.name: Aiwei
characters.description: AiweiBiographical informationNationalityZaofu,Earth KingdomEthnicityEarth KingdomResidenceFog of Lost Souls,Spirit WorldAgec. 60 inBook Three: Change[2]Bornc. 111AGPhysical descriptionEye colorGreenHair colorGrayPersonal informationPronounsHe/him[3]EnemiesSuyin Beifong,Lin Beifong,Team Avatar,ZaheerWeapon of choiceEarth, metalFightingstylesEarthbending,metalbendingPolitical informationProfessionAdviser of Suyin Beifong(formerly)PositionTruth seerZaofu fugitive(formerly)AffiliationMetal Clan(formerly)Red Lotus(formerly)Chronological informationFirst appearance"The Metal Clan"Last appearance"The Stakeout"Voiced byMaurice LaMarcheImage gallery (7)Aiweiwas a former trusted adviser ofZaofu's matriarch,Suyin Beifong, turnedEarth Kingdomfugitive, as well as a very capabletruth seer. Because of this ability, he gained much respect in Zaofu, until he was discovered to be an agent for theRed Lotusafter they failed to kidnapAvatarKorra.[3]After his escape, Aiwei metZaheerin theSpirit World, where he was sentenced by theairbenderto spend an eternity dwelling inside theFog of Lost Souls.[2] In 171AG, a few weeks afterHarmonic Convergence, Aiwei personally greeted Avatar Korra and her companions, who had come to Zaofu in search of a new airbender. When Korra claimed that she and her three friends were the only ones to have arrived, Aiwei detected that she was lying. However, he did not call her out on that until after they met with Suyin, at which point Korra was forced to confess that they came withRepublic City'sChief of Police,Lin Beifong.[1] The next day, Aiwei approached Lin as she was hassling two guards who were chitchatting. He noted that she was visibly stressed and offered the business card of anacupuncturistto help resolve her issues.[4]Subsequently, Aiwei would meditate into the Spirit World and confer with his fellow Red Lotus member Zaheer, passing on Korra's current location at Zaofu to the criminal airbender after he had failed to find the Avatar in Republic City.[5] Aiwei was a generally calm and composed person capable of gaining people's trust, while sporting a welcoming and humble demeanor.[1]These traits landed him the position of adviser and truth seer in the city of Zaofu, which enabled him as the sole person in the city to keep a secret. Underneath this facade, he sported a more calculated side, which enabled him to use the people around him to serve his own machinations and the agenda of the Red Lotus.[3]

characters.name: Akanah
characters.description: AkanahBiographical informationNationalityRepublic City,United RepublicResidenceDragon Flats borough, Republic CityPersonal informationPronounsShe/her[1]AlliesRuo LingPolitical informationProfessionTeacherPositionHeadmistress of the Dragon Flats Reform SchoolAffiliationDragon Flats Reform SchoolChronological informationFirst appearanceAvatar Legends: The Roleplaying Game-Republic City(only appearance)Akanahwas the headmistress of the Dragon Flats Reform School. She hoped to run aninstitutionwhich could truly provide opportunities for some of Republic City's worst students in the underprivilegedDragon Flats borough.[1][2] Before the Dragon Flats Reform School was established, Akanah was a local teacher in the borough. When socialite Ruo Ling announced she wanted to spend money on a school for some of the city's worst students, Akanah agreed to build and govern the school with her. Akanah was aware that Ruo Ling simply wanted to use the project to bolster her celebrity status and dodge taxes. However, she realized that the plan would still give her access to Ruo's resources, with an opportunity to truly build an institution of value for Dragon Flats. Akanah did not have enough funding from Ruo to spend on everything, and looked to spend what she could on renovating important aspects of the school building. She wanted to tear down the vining kudzu covering the school, but Ruo thought it made it look distinguished. Akanah used the funds to hire the best teachers she could find. Many of the teachers she found were ostracized or exiled from other academies, not out of incompetence, but because they protested poor conditions, or made an enemy of a wealthy parent.[1]Akanah was able to quietly direct some of Ruo's money from vanity projects to real improvements to the school, such as using the funds she promised would spend on a large monument to the founder to go to hiring teachers for two new classrooms instead.[2]

characters.name: Akataa
characters.description: AkataaBiographical informationNationalityJasmine IslandEthnicityJasmine IslanderPersonal informationPronounsThey/them[1]AlliesAiko,Basalt,Siku,Fu Meili,Lu TenEnemiesYueya,ZhaoWeapon of choiceStaffPolitical informationProfessionSoldierPositionLeaderof theJasmine Island protectorsAffiliationJasmine Island protectorsChronological informationFirst appearanceUncle Iroh's Adventure Guide-Vengeance & Virtue(only appearance)Akataawas theleaderofJasmine Island'sprotectorsduring the lateHundred Year War. They played a major role in defending the island's people from theFire Nationmilitaryduring "The Devastation". Akataa joined the protectors' ranks at an early point in their life and trained in accordance to the group's traditions.[2]Over the course of the Hundred Year War, however, it had become normal for Jasmine Island protectors to ultimately leave their home to fight as volunteers against theFire Nation.[2][3]As a result, Akataa's older trainers also left, passing the responsibility of training and commanding the remaining, younger protectors to Akataa.[2] Over time, Akataa became one of the most experienced warriors on the island.[1]They took their leadership role seriously, yet often struggled to assert themselves due to still regarding themselves as rather inexperienced.[2]Akataa faced a true test of their abilities when a strong Fire Nation force led byAdmiralYueyaattacked Jasmine Island, launching a murderous campaign later dubbed "The Devastation". After the initial surprise attacks led byCaptainZhao, the surviving protectors of the island rallied around Akataa, and tried to fight off the invaders.[1]Together with their comradesAiko,Basalt,Siku, andFu Meili,[1][3]Akataa later encountered Fire Nation soldiers led by CaptainLu Ten, who actually opposed the slaughter. The protectors allied with Lu Ten,[4][5]and together worked out a plan to stop Yueya.[4][6]This culminated in a battle where Akataa and their friends defeated the admiral, helping to save their home.[4][7] Akataa was a warm and friendly person, making it occasionally difficult for them to make hard and assertive choices as a leader. When they overcame their doubts, however, Akataa was an inspiring and diplomatic person who was well suited to bear both the burdens as well as triumphs of leadership.[2]

characters.name: Akemi
characters.description: AkemiBiographical informationNationalityRepublic City,United RepublicEthnicityFire NationPersonal informationPronounsHe/him[1]EnemiesBadgermoleTriadWeapon of choiceFireFighting styleFirebendingPolitical informationProfessionSmugglerChronological informationFirst appearanceAvatar Legends: The Roleplaying Game-Republic City(only appearance)Akemiwas afirebenderwho ran afood-smuggling ring in and out ofRepublic City, usingthe Warrensof theRepublic City undergroundas his base of operations.[2] Akemi grew up poor and unhoused on the streets ofTin Town, a community of outcasts and unhoused people. His business started with him smuggling fresh food in, as Tin Town had a market hungry for reliable nutrition. He then expanded his ring to include exotic and endangered offerings for Republic City's rich and eclectic. This included glowing lobster crab,cactus juice, andturtle duckshell. Akemi now carefully monitors every agricultural pipeline coming in and out of Republic City. He has contacts with hundreds of vendors and is clever with his business.[2]He has increasingly been locking horns with theBadgermole Triad, a ragtag group of street rats who see the badgermole tunnels as theirs. While the triad mostly tried to run automobile races in the tunnels, they increasingly began to believe that they must oust Akemi to survive. The Badgermole Triad's leader,Hibiki, tried to actively quash Akemi's smuggling ring. Akemi has grown increasingly annoyed at the triad's incursions, and hates that he is being outsmarted by children a quarter of his age.[2][1] Akemi was vengeful, whip-smart, and extravagant. He loved to make an example out of his enemies, and to look good doing so. He was frustrated to no end by the Badgermole Triad, as he hated being outmaneuvered by children, and he struggled to keep his composure whenever he was reminded of this fact. Akemi would go to extraordinary lengths to kneecap any potential disruptions to his enterprise. Akemi was ambitious, and wants to ultimately own Republic City from the shadows. He believed that food is the way to achieve this, as his impoverished origins led him to believe that a good meal is the best way to control someone.[2]

characters.name: Akuudan
characters.description: AkuudanBiographical informationNationalitySouthern Water TribeEthnicityWater TribeResidenceJonduri,Fire NationPhysical descriptionHeight"Giant"[1]Personal informationPronounsHe/him[1]Love interestTayagum(husband)AlliesAvatarYangchen,Kavik,JujintaEnemiesChaisee,Kalyaan,Shang merchants,Thapa,ZongduHensheWeapon of choiceWaterFighting styleWaterbendingPolitical informationProfessionWarriorInnkeeperAffiliationChiefOyaluk(formerly)Water TribesYangchen's networkChronological informationFirst appearance"A Place to Lay One's Head"Last appearance"The Path Forward"Akuudanwas a trusted warrior of theWater Tribes, serving in an important role forChiefOyalukduring thePlatinum Affair, along with his husbandTayagum. After being imprisoned in theEarth Kingdomwhen the international blunder began to unfold, Akuudan and Tayagum became trustedcontacts and alliesofAvatarYangchen, and helped her investigate and bring an end to theUnanimity project. Akuudan was originally from theSouthern Water Tribe,[1]and the cousin ofOyaluk, Chief of both Water Tribes.[2]He displayed the genetic quirk[1]shared by several members of the royal family who were born with only one arm.[1]As a highly capable fighter as well as a relative to the chief,[2]Akuudan served in a high-profile, secretive military capacity for the Water Tribes that involved direct orders from Chief Oyaluk in theNortherncapital ofAgna Qel'a. At some point, he met andmarriedTayagum, a fellow warrior who hailed from the Orca Islands. They modified the traditional custom of giving a hand-carvednecklaceby both wearing betrothal armbands; Akuudan made Tayagum use all of his failed attempts at carving to use as stones on his own armband.[1] During the Platinum Affair, Akuudan and Tayagum served as expedition quartermasters, escorting the Water Tribe's payment of platinum ingots toGeneralNongunder diplomatic pretenses. However, they were run down byEarth KingFeishan's forces atLlamapaca's Crossing, along with the platinum they were guarding. The Water Tribe ambassadors could not get them released right away, and eventually, they stopped trying. The two warriors were disavowed by Oyaluk, and were only freed when Avatar Yangchen personally took it upon herself to negotiate amnesty for them. The husbands became firm allies of Yangchen, believing her to be a person who would help others without question. After being freed, they came to run a Water Tribe innlocatedin theshangcity ofJonduriwhich could act as a safe house for the Avatar's allies.[1] Akuudan initially appeared less expressive and more stern than Tayagum. In order to not blow Kavik's cover, he was willing to break his assistant's nose. However, he actually had the kinder personality out of the husbands. Akuudan greatly revered Yangchen and respectfully referred to her as the Avatar while speaking to her. He followed her not out of a deal or because she was the Avatar, but because she would help others without question. However, he also believed she could be too tru
characters.personality_section: Akuudan initially appeared less expressive and more stern than Tayagum. In order to not blow Kavik's cover, he was willing to break his assistant's nose. However, he actually had the kinder personality out of the husbands. Akuudan greatly revered Yangchen and respectfully referred to her as the Avatar while speaking to her. He followed her not out of a deal or because she was the Avatar, but because she would help others without question. However, he also believed she could be too trusting of people and capable of mistakes as a result.[1]He was very protective of Yangchen and gave Kavik a glare for touching and embracing the Avatar. He was also very fond of his husband, having genuine warm expressions around him, affectionally referring to him as "dumb-dumb" and keeping all the failed betrothal necklace stones he carved. The two sometimes immaturely joked around together, and they looked forward to retiring. After Kavik betrayed them and Jujinta tried to stab him, Akuudan protected the boy, because he was still working for Yangchen. Akuudan also felt more sympathetic toward the youngwaterbenderbecause he had picked his family over everything else, as Akuudan himself was abandoned by his cousin. He wished more people thought the same way about family.[13]He also envied the way Kalyaan eventually chose his own brother's safety over his mission.[19] Akuudan gently calmed down his husband whenever he felt very angry toward Kavik, but also showed his own disapproval of Kavik's bet

characters.name: Amak
characters.description: AmakBiographical informationNationalityAgna Qel'a,Northern Water TribeEthnicityWater TribeResidenceYokoya,Earth KingdomDied296BGPhysical descriptionEye colorPale blue[1]Personal informationPronounsHe/him[1]AlliesJianzhu,Hei-Ran,Kelsang,Yun,KyoshiEnemiesFifth Nation,TagakaWeapon of choiceWaterFighting styleWaterbendingPolitical informationProfessionAssassin(formerly)Waterbending instructorPositionWaterbending masterAffiliationContract employerChronological informationFirst appearance"The Iceberg"Last appearanceAvatar: GenerationsAmakwas an assassin andwaterbendingmaster from theNorthern Water Tribewho worked in theEarth Kingdomduring the late 4th and early 3rd centuryBG. He eventually became the waterbending instructor of the falsely identifiedAvatar,Yun, though was killed during a confrontation with theFifth Nationpirates. Amak hailed from the Northern Water Tribe's capitalAgna Qel'a, and was the brother of the renowned master healerAtuat.[2]He became proficient as a hired killer, excelling in disguise, poisoning, and combat. Around 306 BG, he left his home and began working in theEarth Kingdom,[1]where he took part in theWars of Secrets and Daggers.[3]During these conspiratorial conflicts, Amak found employment for a lesser prince inBa Sing Se. This nobleman consequently jumped from the eleventh to the fourth place in the line of succession, as several members of the Earth Kingdom's royal family unexpectedly died. Amak was eventually hired byJianzhuto train the false Avatar Yun in waterbending.[1]Having learned numerous techniques of assassination during his time in Ba Sing Se,[3]Amak used this opportunity to also train Jianzhu and Yun to resist various poisons as well as how to use them,[3][4][5]although his poison training with Yun did not progress very far.[6] Serving as Yun's waterbending teacher, Amak and his charge spent quite some time together during their regular training sessions. Although Amak did not hold back during those sessions and regularly rendered Yun bloody and mussed at the end of them, he held Yun's respect and was lauded by him for being his favorite teacher next to Jianzhu. Amak's style placed more emphasis on the functionality of bending than its form. Amak was a reserved and withdrawn person who seemed impenetrable and mysterious to others. He had a frown that, added to his appearance, put many people off. Amak prioritized function over form in his teaching. During demonstrations of weak forms of waterbending, Amak appeared mocking the person without particularly showing it.[1]

characters.name: Amihan
characters.description: AmihanBiographical informationNickname"Ami"[1]NationalityLambak IslandEthnicityLambak clanAge"Middle-aged" inThe Reckoning of Roku[2]Bornc. 110sBGPhysical descriptionEye colorBlue[2]Hair colorBlack[3]Personal informationPronounsShe/her[2]AlliesUlo,Mamamaril, KamaoEnemiesMalaya, Kilat,Gyatso,Roku,Western Kingdom Trading CompanyWeapon of choiceAir, knifeFighting styleAirbendingPolitical informationProfessionScoutChiefPositionChief of the Lambak clanReign63 BGPredecessorBaku(as chief)AffiliationLambak clanChronological informationFirst appearance"Knowledge to Destroy"Last appearance"The Teeth of the Trap"Amihanwas anairbendingscout and laterchiefof the isolationistLambak clanduring the 1st centuryBG. An experienced killer, she embraced her covert role as scout to murder any outside intruders on her home island to keep her clan safe from threats. As a result of her ruthlessness, she was a trusted agent of the clanchiefat the time,Ulo. When several groups of foreigners landed on Lambak Island in 66 BG, Amihan happilyfought against themas well as her former comradeMalaya, a local scout who opposed Ulo. In the process, the airbender also became a major enemy ofAvatarRokuandGyatsowho were among the foreign intruders. Ultimately, the airbender was defeated by Malaya and Gyatso, but managed to escape and went into hiding. Amihan later returned to become chief herself, waging a brutal but ultimately failed rebellion against the island'sFire Nationoccupiers. Amihan was a cruel and even psychopathic person who felt no remorse over killing.[2][3]She tormented and killed both animals as well as foreign people just to test her airbending,[2][3]and treated murder as a casual occurrence.[3]According to Malaya, the airbender had a "twisted sense of humor",[2]considering it amusing when she toyed with her opponents before killing them or exposed her allies to mortal danger.[3]Amihan also relished her fight with Malaya, happy that one of her targets finally represented an actual challenge. Despite her cruel nature, she also felt loyalty to her clan, believing that her actions protected them.[7]

Their ultimate goal was to introduce equality for all by permanently ridding theworldofbenders.[7]A nimble, agile, and stealthy fighter, Amon was fearless in facing his opponents, regardless of their powerful bending skills and abilities.[8] Claiming to be an innocent nonbender to whom the spirits granted the ability to remove a person's bending permanently, a power previously demonstrated only by theAvatar, Amon gained the confidence and support of many people with the same ideals. However, when the complete conquest ofRepublic Cityby the Equalists became imminent, Amon's true identity was exposed to the public by AvatarKorra. In actuality, he was a prodigiousNorthern Water Tribebloodbender, son of the escaped crime boss,Yakone. Alongside his younger brother, the formercouncilman,Tarrlok, Amon was unknowingly shaped by Yakone to be a tool for his revenge against the Avatar. With his identity exposed and his followers deserting him, Amon fled the city with Tarrlok, but the latter ignited the fuel tank of theirspeedboat, killing them both.[9] Noatak andTarrlokwere happier during their younger years. As a child, Noatak was caring and fair-minded. He strove to treat everyone equally and protected his younger brother, Tarrlok, from their father's verbal abuse. However, after being corrupted by bloodbending and enduring the burden of Yakone's expectations for several years, Noatak grew cold and distant. He assumed a more detached persona and showed disregard for the suffering of his bloodbending victims, including his own brother. Eventually, Noatak fully embraced both his power and his independence by forcibly halting his father's abuse of Tarrlok, flaunting his power over him, and denouncing him as weak.[4] Unlike Tarrlok, Amon reveled in his
characters.personality_section: As a child, Noatak was caring and fair-minded. He strove to treat everyone equally and protected his younger brother, Tarrlok, from their father's verbal abuse. However, after being corrupted by bloodbending and enduring the burden of Yakone's expectations for several years, Noatak grew cold and distant. He assumed a more detached persona and showed disregard for the suffering of his bloodbending victims, including his own brother. Eventually, Noatak fully embraced both his power and his independence by forcibly halting his father's abuse of Tarrlok, flaunting his power over him, and denouncing him as weak.[4] Unlike Tarrlok, Amon reveled in his bloodbending abilities. He idolized Avatar Aang's ability to remove an individual's bending, believing such a skill to be even more powerful than bloodbending. In time, Amon devised his own technique to sever a bender's connection to their element through bloodbending, an action only known to be reversible with energybending.[4] Amon harbored a severe abhorrence of benders and regarded bending as an "impurity" to be "cleansed". characters.name: Amrita
characters.description: AmritaBiographical informationNationalityRepublic City,United RepublicEthnicityEarth KingdomPersonal informationPronounsShe/her[1]AlliesJargala Omo,Creeping Crystal TriadWeapon of choiceEarthFighting styleEarthbendingPolitical informationProfessionMobsterPositionLieutenant of the Creeping Crystal TriadMaster earthbenderAffiliationCreeping Crystal TriadChronological informationFirst appearanceMovers & Shakers(only appearance)Amritawas a lieutenant of theCreeping Crystal Triad. She focused on extorting people in themoverindustry, as she loved films and aspired to be a rich and famous director.[2][3] As a lieutenant ofJargala Omo, Amrita performed well as a mobster in the Creeping Crystal Triad. However, her real dream was always to leave her life of crime and break into the mover industry as a director. Amrita had been enamored with movers since the first one she saw, and she started looking for a way in, however she could get there.[2][3] When the triad gave a loan toHongmeito start his own mover company, Amrita kept an eye on him as a source for more "fruitful" business endeavors.[1]However, Hongmei did all he could to cut ties and keep Amrita out, which she took personally.[3] Amrita was a very forceful individual. While she had a dream of breaking into the mover industry, she was willing to be ruthless to do so.[3]

characters.name: Anada
characters.description: AnadaBiographical informationNationalityAir NomadsEthnicityFire NationPersonal informationPronounsShe/her[1]AlliesHer grandmotherPolitical informationProfessionEngineerNunChronological informationFirst appearanceAvatar Legends: The Roleplaying Game-Core Book(only appearance)Anadawas anAir Nunand greatengineerwho lived during the era ofRoku. She was born a nonbender in theFire Nation, and was adopted by the Air Nomads as a baby.[1] Anada was born in the Fire Nation and was orphaned as a baby. She was initially raised by her grandmother, but when she could no longer take care of the child, Anada was given over to the Air Nomads and raised as one of their own. She lived in a time when the Air Nomads were more involved with the worldly affairs of the otherfour nationsthan they had been previously. Anada became a great engineer, whose innovative designs created theFire & Air Center of Learning, which celebrated the support of Air Nomad teachings by the Fire Nationnobility. This allowed her the chance to return to the nation where she was born and reunite with her beloved grandmother. However, shortly after arriving at the center, work halted when theGuiding Windbegan to interfere and the Fire Nationmilitaryguard seized control of the site.[1]

characters.name: Analay
characters.description: AnalayPhysical descriptionEye colorPurpleHair colorBlack with red highlightsSkin colorPalePersonal informationPronounsShe/her[1]AlliesRiley,Hiroshi,Visola, Genji,Yahshi,Team AvatarWeapon of choiceShadowsFighting styleShadowbendingPolitical informationAffiliationUnnamed teamChronological informationFirst appearance"New Recruits"(only appearance)Image gallery (1)Analaywas abenderwho specialized in a unique form of bending called shadowbending.[1] Analay was one of the four benders thatYahshiscouted forTeam Avatar. After showing off her unique shadowbending abilities and ticklingAvatarAangwith shadows, the gang began to discuss whether or not to recruit her. However, just as they were about to announce their decision, Analay and the three other benders left to start their own group after deciding that they did not like being judged.[1] Analay is a shadowbender capable of fashioning her shadow intoweaponssuch as whips to bind people from a distance. She is also capable of creating shadow constructs that she can control using her abilities, as she created a shadow wolf with which she travels.[1]

characters.name: Aniki
characters.description: AnikiBiographical informationNationalityGrey Wolf Village,Southern Water TribeEthnicityWater TribePersonal informationPronounsShe/her[1]AlliesMinaq,TeekWeapon of choiceCable-backed bowFighting styleArcheryChronological informationFirst appearanceThe Forbidden Scroll(only appearance)Anikiwas a cautious archer and warrior from theSouthern Water Tribewho fled her home after it was attacked by theFire Nationduring theHundred Year War.[1] Aniki was a child when she had to flee Grey Wolf Village in the Southern Water Tribe with her friendTeekand her baby brother Minaq strapped to her back. Her home was attacked by Fire Nationsoldierslooking forwaterbenders, and Minaq had shown a proficiency for bending at an early age. Aniki knew she could never return home, otherwise she would lose her brother and endanger her family, so the only thing she could do was keep moving. The group left the Southern Water Tribe for the othernations, and eventually came to the Fire Nation itself. In 95AC, when they came to theFire Nation Capital, Teek told Aniki about a scroll that could expose the Fire Nation for what it was. Aniki thought it was a terrible idea to break into theDragonbone Catacombsfor this scroll, but decided to do it anyway to protect Teek, and to get revenge on the villains which had taken so much from her.[1]

characters.name: Animal control officer
characters.description: Animal control officerBiographical informationNationalityBa Sing Se,Earth KingdomEthnicityEarth KingdomPhysical descriptionHair colorBlackPersonal informationPronounsHe/him[1]AlliesThe butcherEnemiesMomo, threepygmy pumasPolitical informationProfessionOfficerChronological informationFirst appearance"The Tales of Ba Sing Se"(only appearance)Voiced byDee Bradley BakerTheanimal control officerwas anEarth Kingdomnational who lived inBa Sing Se. Expressing a certain distaste for animals, he would find and imprison as many straycreaturesas he could, before traveling around theLower Ringselling them to butchers.[1] The animal control officer capturedMomoand threepygmy pumasand attempted to sell them to a butcher, who intended to use them asfood. However, before this deal was finalized, Momo was able to open his cage, as the animal control officer had left the key in the lock, and escape along with the pygmy pumas, whom he freed as well. The officer and butcher soon returned to the cells to find them empty.[2]

characters.name: Anri
characters.description: AnriBiographical informationNationalityEarth KingdomEthnicityEarth KingdomPersonal informationPronounsShe/her[1]AlliesLulu,Peng,Maolo,Flying Koi performersWeapon of choiceEarthFighting styleEarthbendingPolitical informationProfessionArtistCarnival performerCriminalPositionApprentice to MaoloAffiliationFlying Koi CarnivalChronological informationFirst appearanceThe Vanishing Act(only appearance)Anriwas an artist and a performer who was part of theFlying Koi Carnival.[1] Anri always dreamed of joining the circus, and joined the Flying Koi Carnival. She was always seen accompanied by her trainedelephant rat, Lulu. Despite her proximity in age toMaolo, she joined the carnival as his apprentice, and used herearthbendingskills to manipulate colorful clays during hiswaterbendingperformances. She later graduated to being Maolo's partner-in-crime, but she hadn't been able to shake her image as a doting apprentice. Unlike Maolo, Anri was completely sold on RingmasterPeng's mission to steal from rich customers to feed the poor in theLower Ring, despite how ruthless he had become, and despite Maolo confiding his worries in her.[1] Anri was guided by her drive to make a name for herself.[1]
characters.personality_section: Anri was guided by her drive to make a name for herself.[1]

characters.name: Aoma
characters.description: AomaBiographical informationNationalityYokoya,Earth KingdomEthnicityYokoyanBornc. 312BGPhysical descriptionEye colorLight greenHair colorBrownSkin colorLightPersonal informationLove interestYun(crush, formerly)[1]AlliesHer parents,Suzu,JaeEnemiesKyoshi,RangiWeapon of choiceEarthFighting styleEarthbendingPolitical informationProfessionFarmerPositionDaughter of Yokoya's village captainAffiliationYokoyaChronological informationFirst appearance"Nine Years Later"Last appearanceAvatar: GenerationsAomawas anearthbender, farmer, and the daughter of the village captain ofYokoya. In her youth, she was among those who regularly harassed a local servant girl,Kyoshi, due to her closeness to the suspectedAvatar,Yun, despite her status as outsider and orphan. Aoma was born to the village captain of Yokoya and her husband around 312BG. As a result, she was the ideal age to be the possible successor ofAvatarKuruk. When mastersJianzhuandKelsangvisited Yokoya in 305 BG, Aoma was among theEarth Kingdomchildren presented with theAvatar relics, but she failed the test, much to the annoyance of her mother.[2] Growing up, Aomalearnedsome basic earthbending techniques to help her parents with the farm work. Over time, she came to bully Kyoshi, an orphan who had become a servant at theAvatar mansionthat had been built in Yokoya to house Yun, the earthbender identified as Kuruk's successor. Aoma was jealous of Kyoshi's friendship with Yun and the comparatively good life she had at the mansion, while she had to toil on her parents' fields.[3]Her anger at Kyoshi only increased as Aoma developed a crush on Yun.[3][1]Aoma had physically harmed Kyoshi with a rock on many occasions in her youth.[3] Aoma was a selfish and unsympathetic individual. She frequently harassed Kyoshi throughout her youth; on one occasion she did so out of jealousy due to the fact that Kyoshi was close to Yun. Aoma was also defiant, such as when she tossed the clay vessel that Kyoshi had carried high into the air when Rangi confronted her for bullying the servant girl.[3]She was also entitled, dismissing the fact that at least seven local boys had developed a crush on her, as "those village boys have got nothing on Avatar Yun".[1]

characters.name: Appa
characters.description: 阿柏[2]AppaBiographical informationNationalityEastern Air TempleEthnicityAir NomadPhysical descriptionEye colorBrownHair colorWhite and brownSkin typeFurPersonal informationPronounsHe/him[3]AlliesTeam Avatar, Air Nomads,Kyoshi Warriors,Pathik, more ...EnemiesAzula,Ozai,Combustion Man,Zhao,Long Feng, theDai Li,Shuzumu,circus trainer,Ghashiun,Si Wong tribes,beetle-headed merchants, more ...Weapon of choiceAir, body bulk, tailFighting styleAirbendingPolitical informationPositionAang's sky bison(animal guide)PredecessorFang(as previous animal guide of theAvatar)SuccessorNaga(as next animal companion of theAvatar)AffiliationAir NomadsTeam AvatarChronological informationFirst appearance"The Boy in the Iceberg"Last appearanceAvatar: Tea, Tails and Tides: Story FourVoiced byDee Bradley BakerImage gallery (153)AppawasAvatarAang's loyalsky bison, who shared a strong bond with him andMomoas they were all some of the few remaining traces of theAir Nomadcivilization.[4]Appa was most noted for his ability to fly in spite of his heavy weight, a feat achieved throughairbending, which made him the primary means oftransportationutilized byTeam Avatar.[5] Appa andAangmet when thebisonwas a mere calf. When Appa was still a young calf who lived around theEastern Air Temple, young airbenders were allowed to meet their future companions, who would bond with them for life. The herd'smotherwillingly directed her calves to meet the young monks. Aang came forward and offered an apple to Appa, and the two became best friends almost immediately afterward.[1] Appa seemed to have a reasonably trusting attitude, as he did not take long to accept the apple offered to him by Aang - to whom he became incredibly loyal -[1]and took an immediate liking to Sokka when he first saw him.[3]However, this trait was damaged due to his experience with thecircus trainer[1]and the sandbenders.[1][22]In these difficult moments, he seemed to have similar feelings toward humans when he missed Aang and his family in the Eastern Temple[1]while he tried his best to find Team Avatar. Appa had some degree of intelligence, yawning after Aang prompted him[23]and apparently "scolded"Hawkyand Momo when the two fought due to Sokka's order that himself was in charge.[24]His intelligence was further shown when he was taking the team and ProfessorZeitoWan Shi Tong's library, responding with a groan when Zei asked him a question.[22] Appa was afraid of going underground.[25][26]Since his experience in the Fire Nation circus, he also developed a fear of fire.[1]

characters.name: Appa's mother
characters.description: Appa's motherBiographical informationNationalityEastern Air TempleEthnicityAir NomadPhysical descriptionHair colorWhite and brownSkin typeFurPersonal informationAlliesAir NomadsWeapon of choiceAir, body bulkFighting styleAirbendingPolitical informationPositionMother sky bisonAffiliationAir NomadsChronological informationFirst appearance"Appa's Lost Days"(flashback only)(only appearance)Voiced byDee Bradley BakerAppa's mother[1]was theflying bisonwho gave birth toAppaand four other flying bison, with whom she lived at theEastern Air Temple. She was large compared to her children, who were less than one tenth of her size.[2] Appa's mother appeared to her son in a dream when he was feeling alone: She was flying along at the Eastern Air Temple with her five calves circling around her, heading toward SisterIioand severalAir Nomadchildren, among whom stoodAvatarAang. She nuzzled her children tenderly before willingly directing them to the platform where the Air Nomad boys were awaiting them.[2]
